title: The Summit at Golden Hour
bodyText: We started the ascent at 3am to catch the light. Worth every step.

photos.alt: Snow-capped mountain peak at sunrise
photos.caption: First light cresting the ridge — we'd been waiting in the cold for 40 minutes.

photos.alt: Rocky trail leading up the ridge
photos.caption: The last exposed section before the summit plateau.

title: Mist Rolling Through the Pines
bodyText: Dense pine forest with morning fog — the kind of quiet that feels earned.

photos.alt: Dense pine forest with morning fog
photos.caption: 7am. The fog hadn't lifted yet and everything felt muted.

metaData.location: Gifford Pinchot National Forest, Washington, USA

photos.alt: Sunlight filtering through tall pine trees
photos.caption: An hour later, shafts of light started breaking through the canopy.

metaData.lens: Fujinon XF 18mm f/1.4 R LM WR
metaData.location: Gifford Pinchot National Forest, Washington, USA

title: High Tide at Crescent Beach
bodyText: Turquoise waves, salt air, and nobody else on the sand at 6am.

photos.alt: Turquoise ocean waves crashing on shore
photos.caption: High tide. The water was further up the sand than I'd ever seen it.

photos.alt: Empty beach at early morning low light
photos.caption: 30 minutes before sunrise — blue hour on an empty beach.

title: Downtown Lights After Midnight
bodyText: City skyline reflected in the river. The stillness after last call is something else.

photos.alt: City skyline reflected in a river at night
photos.caption: 2am. A completely still river makes for a perfect mirror.

photos.alt: Neon signs blurring in a rain-slicked street
photos.caption: Light rain had just stopped — the wet asphalt does all the work.

title: Sahara at Midday
bodyText: Red sand dunes stretching to the horizon. The heat was surreal.

photos.alt: Red sand dunes stretching to the horizon
photos.caption: Midday in the Sahara. The light was brutal and absolutely flat.

photos.alt: Camel tracks cutting across a flat sand plain
photos.caption: The tracks were from that morning's caravan — wind would erase them by afternoon.

title: Hidden Falls Deep in the Rainforest
bodyText: Two hours off-trail to find it. A waterfall nobody had named yet.

photos.alt: Tall waterfall cascading into a jungle pool
photos.caption: Had to use a 3-stop ND to get the silky water effect in full daylight.

metaData.lens: Fujinon GF 23mm f/4 R LM WR

photos.alt: Dense green canopy with shafts of light breaking through
photos.caption: The canopy was so thick it took until noon for any light to reach the forest floor.

metaData.lens: Fujinon GF 110mm f/2 R LM WR

title: Salt Flats at Blue Hour
bodyText: The Bonneville Salt Flats just after sunset. The sky turns the ground into a mirror.

photos.alt: Salt flats reflecting a deep blue sky
photos.caption: 15 minutes after sunset. The reflection was almost perfect.

title: Market Day in Marrakech
bodyText: The medina at 9am. Spice stalls, chaos, colour — and the best mint tea of my life.

photos.alt: Colourful spice stalls in a busy market
photos.caption: The vendor had been here since 4am. He didn't seem tired.

title: Rooftop Portrait, Hanoi
bodyText: Shot on a guesthouse rooftop in the old quarter. The haze does something special to backlight.

photos.alt: Person standing on a rooftop against a hazy city skyline
photos.caption: Midday haze turned the sky into a giant softbox.

title: Tidal Pools, Oregon Coast
bodyText: Low tide exposes a whole other world. Anemones, starfish, the works.

photos.alt: Colourful tidal pool filled with sea anemones
photos.caption: The pool was about knee-deep. Every surface had something living on it.

metaData.lens: Nikkor Z MC 105mm f/2.8 VR S

title: Fog Over Kyoto
bodyText: November fog rolling through the bamboo grove at Arashiyama. Arrived before the tourists.

photos.alt: Bamboo grove disappearing into morning fog
photos.caption: 6:30am. The grove opens at 9. Worth the early alarm.

title: Subway Commute, New York
bodyText: Rush hour on the L train. Everyone in their own world.

photos.alt: Crowded subway car in motion blur
photos.caption: Shot at 1/15s to get motion in the train, keep the subjects sharp.

metaData.location: L Train, Manhattan, New York, USA

title: Milky Way Over the Dolomites
bodyText: Drove three hours into the mountains to get away from light pollution. Completely worth it.

photos.alt: Milky Way arc over jagged mountain silhouettes
photos.caption: 25-second exposure, f/1.8, ISO 6400. No tracker — just luck with the composition.

metaData.location: Tre Cime di Lavaredo, South Tyrol, Italy

title: Tea Plantation, Munnar
bodyText: Rolling green terraces as far as you can see. The workers move fast.

photos.alt: Green tea terraces on rolling hills
photos.caption: The mist hadn't cleared yet. It made the greens impossibly saturated.

title: Winter Light, Reykjavik
bodyText: 4 hours of daylight in January. The sun never gets high enough to be harsh.

photos.alt: Colourful houses on a quiet street in winter light
photos.caption: 1pm and the sun was already setting. The low angle makes every surface glow.

metaData.lens: Fujinon XF 23mm f/1.4 R LM WR

title: Elephant at the Waterhole
bodyText: Kruger National Park, dusk. The elephant came in alone, drank for 20 minutes, left.

photos.alt: Elephant drinking at a waterhole at dusk
photos.caption: ISO 6400 and a 400mm at the limit of usable shutter speed.

metaData.lens: Nikkor Z 400mm f/2.8 TC VR S

title: Golden Gate in the Rain
bodyText: Everyone leaves when it rains. That's when you stay.

photos.alt: Golden Gate Bridge emerging from rain clouds
photos.caption: The towers were in and out of the clouds for an hour. Got lucky on this frame.

metaData.lens: Sony FE 70-200mm f/2.8 GM OSS II

title: Alley Cats, Istanbul
bodyText: Istanbul belongs to its cats. They know it too.

photos.alt: Two cats resting on old stone steps
photos.caption: They'd been there all morning. Not concerned about me at all.

title: Aurora Over Tromsø
bodyText: The forecast said 30% chance. We stayed up anyway. Best decision of the trip.

photos.alt: Green aurora borealis over a snowy fjord
photos.caption: It started as a faint glow and then exploded. We were screaming.

title: Wet Season Rice Fields, Bali
bodyText: The terraces are greenest in January. Tourists mostly avoid the rain — lucky for me.

photos.alt: Bright green rice terraces after rain
photos.caption: Shot right after a downpour. The water on the leaves was catching everything.

metaData.lens: Fujinon GF 32-64mm f/4 R LM WR

title: Harbour at Low Tide, Nova Scotia
bodyText: Peggy's Cove at low tide. The boats rest at odd angles on the exposed rock.

photos.alt: Fishing boats resting on rocks at low tide
photos.caption: The light at 7am hit the hull paint perfectly.

title: Temple at Dawn, Angkor
bodyText: Angkor Wat at sunrise is a cliché. It's a cliché because it works.

photos.alt: Temple spires reflected in a still pond at sunrise
photos.caption: The reflection pool was perfectly still for about 10 minutes. Then the wind came.

title: Street Food, Bangkok
bodyText: Yaowarat Road at midnight. The woks never stop.

photos.alt: Street vendor cooking over a flaming wok at night
photos.caption: The flame came up every 30 seconds. I was ready on the third one.

title: Abandoned Greenhouse, Berlin
bodyText: Found it on the outskirts of the city. Glass still intact, plants long gone.

photos.alt: Overgrown interior of an abandoned glass greenhouse
photos.caption: The light through dirty glass made every corner interesting.

metaData.lens: Fujinon XF 18mm f/1.4 R LM WR

title: Long Exposure, Faroe Islands
bodyText: Múlafossur waterfall straight into the ocean. Wind was brutal. Tripod nearly went over.

photos.alt: Waterfall pouring off a cliff into the ocean
photos.caption: 6-second exposure. Took 40 attempts to get one without camera shake.

title: Port of Hong Kong After Rain
bodyText: The harbour looks best when everything is wet. Neon doubles on the water.

photos.alt: Hong Kong harbour at night with neon reflections
photos.caption: The rain stopped 10 minutes before I got here. The puddles lasted just long enough.

title: Canyon Light Shaft, Antelope
bodyText: Upper Antelope Canyon at noon in July. The light beams last about 20 minutes.

photos.alt: Light beam cutting through a narrow sandstone canyon
photos.caption: The guides throw sand into the beam to make it visible. I waited for a natural one.

metaData.lens: Fujinon GF 23mm f/4 R LM WR

title: Fishing Village, Vietnam
bodyText: Ha Long Bay at 5am, before the tour boats. The fishermen were already heading back.

photos.alt: Wooden fishing boats on a misty bay at dawn
photos.caption: The limestone karsts were invisible in the mist. Just the boats and the water.

metaData.location: Ha Long Bay, Quảng Ninh, Vietnam

title: Studio Portrait, Hard Light
bodyText: One bare strobe, no modifier, subject two feet away. Simple and severe.

photos.alt: High contrast studio portrait with hard shadow
photos.caption: Single Profoto B10 at full power, no gel. The shadows do the storytelling.

metaData.location: Studio, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam

title: Iceberg Alley, Newfoundland
bodyText: May is iceberg season. They drift down from Greenland and run aground near St. Anthony.

photos.alt: Large iceberg floating near rocky coastline
photos.caption: It was the size of a five-storey building. The blue under the waterline was unreal.

title: Chinatown Lanterns, Singapore
bodyText: Chinese New Year prep in full swing. Every alley is strung with red.

photos.alt: Red lanterns strung across a narrow street at dusk
photos.caption: The lanterns go up two weeks before the new year. I got there on day one.

metaData.lens: Fujinon XF 23mm f/1.4 R LM WR

title: Glacier Calving, Alaska
bodyText: We heard it before we saw it. A section the size of a house just dropped into the water.

photos.alt: Glacier face with ice falling into the ocean
photos.caption: Shot at 1/2000s to freeze the falling ice. Everything in frame is blue.

title: Window Light Portrait, Lisbon
bodyText: North-facing window on an overcast afternoon. The best light is free.

photos.alt: Person bathed in soft window light
photos.caption: No reflector, no fill. Just the window and a good subject.

title: Monsoon Season, Varanasi
bodyText: The ghats during monsoon. The Ganges rises and the pilgrims don't stop.

photos.alt: Pilgrims on flooded stone steps leading to a river
photos.caption: The water was knee-deep on the lower ghats. People were bathing anyway.

title: Red Rocks, Utah at Sunrise
bodyText: Arches National Park before the crowds. The sandstone goes from rust to fire in minutes.

photos.alt: Red sandstone arch glowing at sunrise
photos.caption: Hiked in by headlamp to get there before the light. 45 minutes of perfect colour.

title: Café in the Rain, Paris
bodyText: A rainy Tuesday in the 11th. The terrasse was empty. I had it to myself.

photos.alt: Empty café terrace on a rainy Paris street
photos.caption: Shot through the window from inside. The condensation was perfect.

title: Sand Dunes, Namib at Sunset
bodyText: Sossusvlei at golden hour. The shadows in the dune ridges go almost black.

photos.alt: Orange sand dunes at sunset with deep shadows
photos.caption: The dunes change colour every few minutes. You have to keep shooting.

title: Cobblestone Alley, Dubrovnik
bodyText: The old town in October, after the cruise ships leave. Finally quiet.

photos.alt: Narrow cobblestone alley between white stone buildings
photos.caption: The last ferry left at 5pm. By 6, the old town was ours.

title: Drone View, Great Barrier Reef
bodyText: The colours only make sense from above. Turquoise, jade, deep navy — all at once.

photos.alt: Aerial view of coral reef patterns in turquoise water
photos.caption: DJI Mavic 3 Pro at 80m. The reef was 2m below the surface here.

title: Old Man Fishing, Mekong
bodyText: He was standing in the same spot when I arrived and when I left two hours later.

photos.alt: Elderly man fishing with a long rod on a wide river
photos.caption: 200mm from the bank. He never looked up.

title: Tulip Fields, Netherlands
bodyText: Lisse in April. The rows go on forever and the colours feel fake.

photos.alt: Endless rows of red and yellow tulips
photos.caption: Shot wide open at f/1.8 to compress the rows and blur the far end.

title: Urban Geometry, Tokyo
bodyText: Shinjuku from above. Every surface is doing something. There's no wasted space.

photos.alt: Overhead view of a busy Tokyo intersection
photos.caption: From the hotel window at 35 floors up. The crossing clears every 90 seconds.

title: Misty Morning, Scottish Highlands
bodyText: Rannoch Moor in October. Mist, heather, a single tree. The clichés are clichés for a reason.

photos.alt: Lone tree on moorland shrouded in morning mist
photos.caption: The mist burned off by 10am. I was there at 7.

title: Night Market, Chiang Mai
bodyText: Sunday Walking Street. A kilometre of stalls, food, live music, and no cars.

photos.alt: Busy night market with string lights overhead
photos.caption: Shot at f/2 to keep ISO manageable. The string lights do the work.

title: Cliff Edge, Moher
bodyText: The Cliffs of Moher in a westerly gale. The wind was strong enough to lean into.

photos.alt: Dramatic coastal cliffs dropping into rough Atlantic seas
photos.caption: The spray was reaching the top. I had to keep wiping the front element.

metaData.location: Cliffs of Moher, County Clare, Ireland

title: Backstreet, Havana
bodyText: The colour everywhere. Every wall, every car, every door. Havana doesn't do neutral.

photos.alt: Colourful colonial buildings on a quiet Havana street
photos.caption: The peeling paint and the colour together — it only works in person.

title: Lavender Fields, Provence
bodyText: July in the Valensole Plateau. You smell it before you see it.

photos.alt: Purple lavender rows converging to a farmhouse in the distance
photos.caption: Shot at 70mm to compress the rows and pull the farmhouse closer.

title: Dawn Patrol Surf
bodyText: Uluwatu at first light. The sets come in every 8 minutes. You learn to wait.

photos.alt: Surfer dropping into a large wave at sunrise
photos.caption: 400mm from the cliff. The window for a shot is about half a second.

metaData.lens: Nikkor Z 400mm f/2.8 TC VR S

title: Dusk at the Acropolis
bodyText: Athens from Philopappos Hill at dusk. The Parthenon lights up amber right on schedule.

photos.alt: The Acropolis lit up at dusk against a deep blue sky
photos.caption: The lights come on at 8:30pm sharp. I had my composition ready at 8.

metaData.lens: Sony FE 70-200mm f/2.8 GM OSS II

title: Flooded Temple, Thailand
bodyText: Wat Niwet Thammaprawat during the annual floods. Monks row between buildings.

photos.alt: Buddhist temple half-submerged in floodwater
photos.caption: The monks were completely unfazed. This happens every year.

metaData.lens: Fujinon GF 32-64mm f/4 R LM WR
